{"id":"project-LICHEN-9mm2","title":"Unbounded hash regex can capture concatenated hex strings","description":"## Location
scripts/analyze-hetero-fleet.py:61

## Description
The regex `[a-fA-F0-9]{8,}` has no upper bound and will greedily match any hex string of 8+ characters. If a log line contains a packet dump, multiple adjacent hashes, or a long hex field, the regex captures the entire substring as a single 'hash', causing cross-reference failures between implementations.

## Failure Scenario
A Rust log contains 'TX: hash=DEADBEEF payload=CAFEBABE01020304'. The regex captures 'DEADBEEFCAFEBABE01020304' (if there's no delimiter) or just 'DEADBEEF' if there is. But if Python logs the same packet as 'hash=deadbeef' with proper separation, they won't match, causing a false 'missing packet' report.

## Category

scripts/analyze-hetero-fleet.py:61

## Description
The regex `[a-fA-F0-9]{8,}` has no upper bound and will greedily match any hex string of 8+ characters. If a log line contains a packet dump, multiple adjacent hashes, or a long hex field, the regex captures the entire substring as a single 'hash', causing cross-reference failures between implementations.

## Failure Scenario
A Rust log contains 'TX: hash=DEADBEEF payload=CAFEBABE01020304'. The regex captures 'DEADBEEFCAFEBABE01020304' (if there's no delimiter) or just 'DEADBEEF' if there is. But if Python logs the same packet as 'hash=deadbeef' with proper separation, they won't match, causing a false 'missing packet' report.
